page: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the B32as_v2 delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities in bzip3. Its memory subsystem also registers a strong PassMark memory score. Most other performance metrics, including multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, software compilation, and static web serving throughput, fall within the average range. For language model tasks, the server achieves strong text generation speeds with small 135M models, but exhibits weak prompt processing performance when handling large 70B models.

As a burstable instance, the B32as_v2 offers a cost-effective profile for workloads that experience intermittent spikes in resource demand rather than continuous high utilization. The combination of 32 burstable vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM makes it suitable for development environments, build servers, low-to-medium traffic web servers, and database operations that can tolerate burstable CPU allocation.
description: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz, configured with 16 physical cores, 32 vCPUs, and 128.0 GB of RAM. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside strong PassMark memory scores. Most other metrics, such as multi-core CPU performance, static web serving, and software compilation, remain in the average tier. Given its burstable CPU allocation, this instance provides a cost-efficient solution for workloads with variable resource demands. It is well-suited for development environments, build servers, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 128.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering balanced performance for variable workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, managed via Hyper-V.
tagline: A burstable x86_64 virtual machine featuring thirty-two AMD EPYC vCPUs and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of RAM for variable workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 burstable vCPUs
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Delivers strong single-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression performance
Configured without local storage but supports Premium Storage options
Suited for development environments, build servers, and variable database workloads
